# AudienceScore

> An open, attestation-gated reputation protocol. Writing a review requires
> cryptographic proof of a verified transaction or verified participation; a
> signed receipt binds a pseudonymous holder (payer or participant role) to a
> versioned offering at an attestation level (L1 TRANSACTED, L2 ENGAGED, L3
> COMPLETED, L4 OUTCOME), and that receipt is the only thing that unlocks a
> review. Published scores are versioned, deterministic renderings over the
> append-only review ledger — dual all-verified/completer views, k-anonymity
> gated — that anyone can recompute. Status: spec v0.2.0 released; pilot is
> live, pre-cryptographic-audit; two release gates remain open (independent
> crypto review, per-vertical legal review). The pilot ledger may be reset and
> receipts re-issued after the audit.

Key facts for agents: scores are distributed as Ed25519-signed manifests
carrying spec version, sample size, locality, a Wilson 95% lower bound, and
a provenance hash of the exact event set used — verify the signature and,
if you mirror the log, recompute the number; trusting the server is never
required. The hosted pilot API exposes REST reads and an HTTP JSON-RPC `get_score`
tool. Reading scores requires no account or API key. Score math is open
forever by constitution; only operator-side admission (anti-fraud) checks
may be sealed, under hash-commit-then-reveal accountability.

## Connect (remote MCP)

A live, public MCP server speaking Streamable HTTP (spec 2025-06-18). Add it by
URL in any MCP client — no account, no API key, read-only:

- MCP endpoint: `https://audiencescore-mcp.vercel.app/mcp`
- Tool: `get_score(vendor_id[, state])` → an Ed25519-signed manifest (percent
  verified thumbs-up, Wilson 95% lower bound, sample size, provenance hash) the
  caller verifies and recomputes from public data. Pilot deployment,
  pre-cryptographic-audit; with no verified reviews yet a vendor returns
  `displayed: false` rather than a fabricated number.
- One-line handshake: `curl -s -X POST https://audiencescore-mcp.vercel.app/mcp -H 'content-type: application/json' --data '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"get_score","arguments":{"vendor_id":"<vendor>"}}}'`

## Pilot API

- [Pilot API](https://api.audiencescore.org): hosted pilot, not production
- [Copy to LLM](https://api.audiencescore.org/docs/copy-to-llm): paste-ready agent integration brief
- [Issuer quickstart](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/docs/pilot/ISSUER-QUICKSTART.md): merchant setup
- [Leave a review](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/docs/pilot/LEAVE-A-REVIEW.md): customer and agent review submission
- [Read scores](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/docs/pilot/READ-SCORES.md): get_score, verification, recomputation

## Specifications

- [Spec v0.2a](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/spec/SPEC-v0.2a.md): the normative protocol — attestation ladder, roles, versioned offerings, receipt schema, invariants, threat model
- [Adversarial review](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/spec/ADVERSARIAL-REVIEW.md): the findings register (F1–F10) behind spec v0.2a
- [Conformance](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/tree/main/conformance): signed test vectors, canonical serialization rules, reference verifier
- [Acceptance tests](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/tests/ACCEPTANCE-TESTS.md): AT-1..AT-25, all executable in CI
- [Rendering v1](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/score-spec/rendering-v1.md): the v0.2 score math, normative
- [Event spec (v0.1)](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/protocol/event-spec.md) (superseded by spec v0.2a; retained): signed, hash-chained verdict events
- [Receipt spec (v0.1)](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/protocol/receipt-spec.md) (superseded by spec v0.2a; retained): proof tiers and single-use review rights
- [Score spec v0.1](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/score-spec/score-spec-v0.1.md) (superseded by spec v0.2a; retained): the v0.1 score math
- [Pilot API OpenAPI](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/protocol/openapi.json): hosted pilot REST and MCP HTTP JSON-RPC paths
- Historical permalink for the superseded v0.1.0 tag: [v0.1.0 tag](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/tree/v0.1.0)

## Reference implementation

- [Overview and quickstart](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/reference-impl/README.md): zero-dependency Node.js; `node reference-impl/demo.js` runs receipt → verdict → score → MCP query end to end
- [MCP server](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/reference-impl/src/mcp-server.js): JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio, `get_score` returns a signed manifest

## Governance and policy

- [Governance constitution](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/GOVERNANCE.md): the open-forever guarantees and sealed-admission accountability
- [Architecture overview](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/docs/architecture.md)
- [Prior art and comparisons](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/docs/prior-art.md)
- [Contributing](https://github.com/audiencescore/audiencescore/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): RFC process for spec changes, DCO for code
